title: Runtime Configuration, Secrets, Environment Precedence, and Sensitive Data · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary
summary: A service must receive database credentials and rotation-safe settings without baking them into images, logs, history, or source control. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker build runtime config boundary increment in configuration and secret-flow contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration.
Compare rootless mode, userns remap, rootful daemon groups, contexts, remote TLS, and socket proxies and document remaining authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary · Theory Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation. Competency probe: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation. Competency probe: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration. A defensible solution applies Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to configuration and secret-flow contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-config-secrets-environment-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-config-secrets-environment-4
    artifact: configuration-boundary.yaml
    decision: worked docker config secrets environment 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      public-config: immutable-environment-map
      runtime-secret: mounted-file
      build-secret: buildkit-secret-mount
      logs: redact-values
      rotation: restart-with-versioned-reference
    verify: docker compose config --environment && docker image history --no-trunc candidate
    failure: Reject secrets in Dockerfile ENV, ARG, Compose interpolation output, image history, repository files, or diagnostic logs.
    repair: Separate public config from secrets, inject each at the narrowest lifetime, scan metadata, rotate the credential, and verify redaction.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: ARG and ENV are equivalent inputs that disappear when the build completes. This fails the Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration.
